skill.name: github-repo-search
skill.description: Searches public GitHub repositories, filters candidates, and produces a structured recommendation report for open source project discovery.
skill.summary: Find and compare public GitHub repositories with structured search, filtering, and ranking guidance.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: The static command-execution and weak-crypto findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code, fenced examples, and ordinary repository metadata field names. No executable code, prompt injection attempt, credential handling, or data exfiltration pattern was found in SKILL.md. The remaining risk is low because the skill requires outbound GitHub search of public repositories, with no user token by default.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged many backtick-delimited strings as Ruby or shell execution. In context, these are Markdown inline code and fenced text examples for filters, fields, and report formats, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown documentation and examples only. SKILL.md contains no script block, interpreter directive, or instruction to execute shell commands.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detection Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to hashing or encryption logic. The referenced lines contain skill metadata and a repository description field name.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter description text and line 110 is the literal field label description. I found no cryptographic API, algorithm selection, or credential processing in the file.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Heuristic Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The file is a UTF-8 Markdown instruction document with Chinese text and structured headings. I found no binary payload, encoded blob, hidden script, or encrypted content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review showed readable Markdown from the first line through the end of the file. The entropy score is consistent with multilingual text rather than obfuscation.

low_findings.title: Outbound GitHub Search Dependency
low_findings.description: The skill is designed to search public GitHub repositories and review repository metadata. This is legitimate for the stated purpose, but it requires network access and may expose user search topics to GitHub.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The workflow explicitly uses GitHub public repositories and API quota limits. The risk is limited because the skill states it does not use a user token by default.

content.value_statement: Choosing open source projects is difficult when search results are noisy and hard to compare. This skill turns a research goal into filtered GitHub candidates with clear ranking, project types, and next steps.
actual_capabilities: Collects and confirms search requirements before starting repository research.
Creates 5 to 10 GitHub search query groups with stated search intent.
Filters public repositories by stars, archive status, language, and relevance criteria.
Classifies repositories by role, such as framework, application, curated list, or research layer.
Produces a structured Top N report with repository metadata and recommendation reasoning.
Supports iterative refinement when results are too broad, too narrow, or not relevant enough.
limitations: Only covers public GitHub repositories and public repository metadata.
Does not use a user GitHub token by default, so API quotas are limited.
Does not install, run, or benchmark the recommended projects unless separately requested.
Search quality depends on the clarity of the confirmed topic and constraints.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Technical Options
use_cases.description: Compare mature open source frameworks for a planned engineering project.

use_cases.title: Build a Research Shortlist
use_cases.description: Find relevant repositories, curated lists, and examples for a new product area.

use_cases.title: Map an Open Source Landscape
use_cases.description: Identify leading projects, project types, and maintenance signals in a technical domain.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Find public GitHub repositories about browser automation. Use the default minimum stars and give me a Top 10 report.

prompt_templates.title: Search With Constraints
prompt_templates.prompt: Find TypeScript repositories for RAG evaluation tools. Prefer active projects, exclude archived repositories, and rank by relevance first.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Project Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Search GitHub for agent memory projects. Separate frameworks, applications, infrastructure, and curated lists in the final report.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Weak Result Set
prompt_templates.prompt: The previous results were too broad. Increase the star threshold to 500, focus on production-ready Python projects, and explain why each result fits.

output_examples.input: Find open source tools for evaluating LLM agents.
output_examples.output: A report with confirmed requirements, search terms, filtering rules, result counts, and a ranked table of repositories with project type, stars, language, license, and recommendation notes.

output_examples.input: Find GitHub projects for local-first vector databases.
output_examples.output: A shortlist that separates database engines, SDKs, examples, and curated resources, with notes on maintenance activity and best-fit use cases.

output_examples.input: Refine the search to active Go projects with at least 500 stars.
output_examples.output: An updated ranking that applies the new language and star filters, explains removed candidates, and highlights the strongest remaining repositories.

best_practices: Confirm topic, result count, ranking mode, and target project type before searching.
Include exclusions such as archived projects, tutorials, demos, or unrelated curated lists.
Ask for refinement when the first report is too broad, too narrow, or misses an important subtopic.
anti_patterns: Do not request a broad topic without a use case or preferred project type.
Do not treat star count as the only quality signal for adoption decisions.
Do not assume the report verifies security, performance, or production readiness by running code.

faq.question: Does this skill search private repositories?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for public GitHub repositories and public metadata.

faq.question: Does it need my GitHub token?
faq.answer: No. The skill states that it does not use a user token by default.

faq.question: Can it filter by programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. You can request a language filter such as Python, TypeScript, Go, or another language.

faq.question: Can it recommend products and frameworks separately?
faq.answer: Yes. It classifies repositories by role, including applications, frameworks, infrastructure, curated lists, and research resources.

faq.question: Does it install or test the repositories?
faq.answer: No. Installation and hands-on validation are outside the default workflow unless you ask for them separately.

faq.question: How does it handle noisy search results?
faq.answer: It uses multiple query groups, removes duplicates, applies hard filters, and reranks results by relevance and project quality signals.
